activities_projects.title: Natural vs. Human-made Sort
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between natural and human-made components of the environment
steps: Cut out pictures of things found in your surroundings (trees, buildings, cars, rivers)
Divide a chart into two columns: 'Natural' and 'Human-made'
Paste the pictures in the correct columns
Identify which items belong to the Biotic and Abiotic categories
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Categorizes different components of the environment correctly
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in sorting the pictures into the two categories (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Biotic and Abiotic Scavenger Hunt
activities_projects.objective: Identify living and non-living things in the immediate environment
steps: Go to a nearby park or your school garden
List 5 living things (Biotic) and 5 non-living things (Abiotic) you see
Observe how the living things depend on the non-living things (e.g., plants needing soil)
Share your findings with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the basic division of the natural environment
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of the list and observation of dependencies (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Four Domains Model
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the four domains of the Earth
materials: Clay or Playdough (Blue, Green, Brown, and White)
steps: Use brown clay to represent Lithosphere (Land)
Use blue clay for Hydrosphere (Water)
Use white/light blue for Atmosphere (Air)
Create a small green zone where all three meet to represent Biosphere (Life)
Label each domain clearly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the four domains and understands that life exists where they interact
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct representation and labeling of the four domains (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: My Local Ecosystem Diary
activities_projects.objective: Observe the interaction between organisms and their surroundings
steps: Find a small ecosystem like a pond, a garden, or even a large tree
Observe the insects, birds, and plants living there
Draw a simple 'Who eats whom' sketch (Food chain)
Write how the animals use the air, water, and shade in that area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Defines an ecosystem through real-world observation
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the food chain sketch and ecosystem description (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Human Environment Evolution Timeline
activities_projects.objective: Learn how humans have changed their environment over time
steps: Draw a picture of early humans living in caves and eating wild fruits
Draw a middle scene showing humans starting to grow crops and domesticate animals
Draw a final scene showing a modern city with cars and factories
Discuss how the 'Barter System' worked in the past vs. money today
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Traces the transition from adapting to nature to modifying nature
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and logical progression of the timeline (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Lithosphere' Treasure Map
activities_projects.objective: Identify features of the hard top layer of the Earth
steps: Draw a map of an imaginary island
Include landforms like mountains, plateaus, and plains
Identify where humans would build houses and where they would farm
Label these features as parts of the Lithosphere
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the importance of the Lithosphere for human habitation
activities_projects.evaluation: Inclusion of various landforms and correct labeling (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Atmosphere and Gravitational Pull
activities_projects.objective: Understand why the atmosphere stays around the Earth
materials: A small piece of light cloth
steps: Hold the ball (Earth) and wrap the cloth loosely around it (Atmosphere)
Try to pull the cloth away
Discuss how the Earth's gravity acts like a magnet to hold the air around it
List two things the atmosphere protects us from (e.g., UV rays, heat)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains the role of the atmosphere and gravity in environment protection
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation in discussion and listing protective functions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Water Sources Survey
activities_projects.objective: Identify different parts of the Hydrosphere
steps: Interview elders at home or neighbors
Ask where the water in your taps comes from (River, Well, Lake, or Dam)
Make a list of all water bodies in or near your town
Classify them into freshwater or saltwater sources
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the diversity of the Hydrosphere in a local context
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed list of local water sources and classification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Environment Slogan Writing
activities_projects.objective: Promote a healthy balance in the environment
steps: Think of a problem in your local environment (like litter or smoke)
Write a catchy slogan to encourage people to protect nature
Decorate it with drawings of trees or animals
Display it on your classroom board
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appreciates the need for a 'Perfect Balance' between nature and humans
activities_projects.evaluation: Effectiveness of the slogan and creativity in design (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Environment Word Search
activities_projects.objective: Reinforce key terms from the chapter
steps: Create a 10x10 grid
Hide 8 words: Biotic, Abiotic, Lithosphere, Atmosphere, Biosphere, Ecosystem, Hydrosphere, Nature
Ask a friend to find and circle the words
Briefly explain the meaning of each word as it is found
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Quick recall and definition of essential environmental terminology
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct placement of words and their definitions (10 marks)
